---
title: "repair_trat2"
output: html_document
date: "2025-05-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse) 
library(magrittr) 
library(tinytex) 
library(readxl) 
library(skimr)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r}
rp.time <- repair %>% group_by(team) %>% summarize(avgtime = mean(repair.time))
rp.time
```
```{r}
summary(repair)
```


```{r}

set.seed(123)

n_repeats <- 10000
result <- numeric(n_repeats)

for (i in 1:n_repeats) {
  permuted_team <- sample(repair$team)
  result[i] <- mean(repair$repair.time[permuted_team == "A"]) - 
                   mean(repair$repair.time[permuted_team == "B"])
}

p_value <- mean(abs(result) >= abs(8.1))
p_value
```
```{r}
set.seed(123) 
n <- length(teamB_times)
rep.boot <- numeric(N)
for (i in 1:N) {
samp <- sample(teamB_times, size = n, replace = T) # draw resample
rep.boot[i] <- mean(samp) # compute mean and store it as my.boo
}
paste("Bootstrap Mean =", round(mean(rep.boot),2))


paste("Bootstrap SE =", round(sd(rep.boot),2))
```
